title: Ambulance Handoff Queue Balancing
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: ambulance handoff queue balancing.
assumptions: Each patient_episode represents a single, discrete ambulance arrival and is processed independently in the model.
Resource reservation operations are atomic and enforce exclusivity while held.
Initial availability of bays, staff, equipment, and slots is accurately represented by the initial facts.
Clinical priority (triage acuity) and external escalation rules exist outside this template and inform compatibility relations.
risks: Delays in resource allocation can increase time-to-treatment and degrade patient safety for high-acuity cases.
Contested resource reservations (e.g., equipment or specialist slots) may cause suboptimal queue balancing and throughput reduction.
Incorrect compatibility assumptions between patient subtype and bed_type or team may result in inappropriate assignments.
Communication failures during handoff (missed checklist steps) can lead to incomplete transfer of critical clinical information.
